title: REQ-FEED-028 Step 2 'existing NoteId set' is incompatible with current scan_vault_feed which keys by full file path, not stem
location: behavioral-spec.md:919-924 (REQ-FEED-028 Detailed Behavior steps 1-4); contrast with promptnotes/src-tauri/src/feed.rs:386-421 (scan_vault_feed)
description: REQ-FEED-028 step 2 prescribes: '既存 NoteId 集合構築: 拡張子を除いたファイル名を `HashSet<String>` に格納する (`existing_ids`)'. The collision check at step 4 then uses this stem-based set. However, the existing `scan_vault_feed` (which the spec implicitly reuses to fill `visible_note_ids` and `note_metadata`) keys notes by the **full filesystem path** — `feed.rs:399-401` does `let note_id = match file_path.to_str() { Some(s) => s.to_string(), None => continue };`. Consequences: (a) the new NoteId returned by `next_available_note_id` is a stem (e.g. `2026-05-13-040000-000`), but the existing `visible_note_ids` are absolute paths (e.g. `/vault/2026-05-13-040000-000.md`). Concatenating `visible_note_ids = [新規ID] + 既存の visible_note_ids` (step 6) produces a list with mixed-namespace identifiers. (b) The collision check that the spec relies on is operating on a different keyspace from the published `note_metadata`, so the invariant `editing.current_note_id == visible_note_ids[0]` (Acceptance Criteria #1) holds only because the new ID is prepended — but `note_metadata.contains_key(visible_note_ids[0])` (PROP-FEED-S5-004) is satisfied by the new entry alone; existing rows in `note_metadata` are still keyed by full path. The spec does not specify whether `scan_vault_feed` must be changed to return stems, nor does it explain how the stem-based collision check interacts with the path-based scan output. The Acceptance Criteria (line 941) claim 'visible_note_ids[1] が既存 ID' without defining what 'existing ID' means in terms of format.
evidence:
behavioral-spec.md:920 — '拡張子を除いたファイル名を `HashSet<String>` に格納する'
behavioral-spec.md:926-928 — 'visible_note_ids = [新規ID] + 既存の visible_note_ids  (既存ノートを末尾に append)'
promptnotes/src-tauri/src/feed.rs:399 — `let note_id = match file_path.to_str() { Some(s) => s.to_string(), None => continue };`
promptnotes/src-tauri/src/feed.rs:415-416 — `visible_note_ids.push(note_id.clone()); note_metadata.insert(note_id, metadata);`
verification-architecture.md:694 — Pure helper signature `next_available_note_id(now_ms: i64, existing: &HashSet<String>) -> String` (no path context)
remediation: Either (a) explicitly require the implementer to change `scan_vault_feed` to use the file stem (`file_path.file_stem()`) as the note_id and amend `visible_note_ids`/`note_metadata` keys accordingly — and add this change as a separate REQ or AC in Sprint 5; or (b) keep current behavior and clarify that `existing_ids` (the stem set used by `next_available_note_id`) is derived from `visible_note_ids` via `Path::new(s).file_stem()` for collision check only, while the published `visible_note_ids` continue to use full paths. In case (b), document the namespace asymmetry explicitly and add an AC that the new ID format does NOT match any existing path-formatted note_id (which it cannot, by virtue of having no '/' or '.md' suffix). The current spec leaves the implementer to guess which interpretation is intended.
